title: Built a efficient and fast MRI compression program called KMRI [P]
author: Reddit r/MachineLearning
summary: KMRI is a chunk-based MRI compression tool designed for .nii files, combining Python and C++ with Zstd for high-speed, efficient compression. It achieves up to ~900× compression on synthetic smooth MRI volumes by skipping zero-valued blocks—a domain-optimized strategy for medical imaging data. The open-source implementation prioritizes both speed and ratio, making it practical for storage and transfer of large neuroimaging datasets.
